own_review_points: CERT_CONTENT attribute defined but never addParameter()'d to DEFINITION => certificate-content unusable via mgmt API
Empty else branch with only '// throw exception' => silent no-op when neither cert-file nor certificate-content supplied
context.getResult().set(info) inside loop immediately overwritten by result.set(mCertificates) — dead/misleading assignment
getAllChannelNames() defined but never called — dead code
Test mock parseCertificate uses while(reader.ready()) — unreliable BufferedReader idiom, should use readLine()!=null
CLI AddCertificatesCommand prints status before checking executeOp result; print vs println
i18n: several handlers log/throw raw strings bypassing InstMgrLogger (human ref)
AddCertificatesCommand: 'cert-file' literal where a constant exists; redundant String.format with no args (human ref)
notes: v8 strong and well-grounded. VERIFIED: CERT_CONTENT defined (setRequired false) but DEFINITION only addParameter(CERT_FILE) — addParameter(CERT_CONTENT) absent => point2 = critical (real API defect). '// throw exception' empty else present => point1 = critical (silent success). point3 result.set(info) overwrite verified = good. point6 getAllChannelNames single occurrence (def only) = good dead-code. point7 while(reader.ready()) verified = good (legit anti-pattern). point8 UpdateCommand SUCCESS-on-decline = good (overlaps human 'should not run if canceled'). points4 (logger needs a 'neither provided' message) and point5 (print vs println / unchecked response) are secondary nits = trivial x2. No fabrications (all files in change list). MISSED distinct human points v8 omitted: i18n missing (InstMgrListUpdates/PrepareRevert/PrepareUpdate), CLI command docs in command_resources.properties, 'cert-file' constant exists, redundant String.format, pom Beta1 outdated, per-handler javadoc. Count 4 missed (clustering i18n as 1, docs 1, cert-file-constant 1, String.format/redundancy 1). net = (4 + 2*2) - 4 = 8 - ... compute: good/crit/wrong/triv sum = 4*1 + 2*2 + 0 + 0 = 8; minus 4 missed = 4.
